name: Romanian Mamaliga with Cheese
steps: Simmer the cornmeal in the milk and 0.5 cup water, stirring constantly, 12-15 minutes until thick.
Stir in the cheese and butter.
steps_detailed: Simmer the cornmeal in the milk and 0.6 cup water over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 14-17 minutes until thick and smooth.
Stir in the cheese and butter until melted, and top with the sour cream.
Mamaliga, cornmeal porridge, is as central to Romanian cooking as bread, eaten at any meal in place of it.
description: Mămăligă, a cornmeal porridge similar to polenta, is a Romanian staple, often paired with cheese and sour cream.

name: Romanian Eggs with Peppers and Onion
steps: Soften the onion in the oil.
Scramble in the eggs and tomatoes, cooking gently until just set.
steps_detailed: Soften the onion in the oil over medium heat, 5-6 minutes.
Scramble in the eggs, tomatoes, and pepper, cooking gently, stirring often, until just set.
description: A simple egg, pepper, and onion breakfast.

name: Romanian Oat Porridge
steps: Simmer the oats in the milk for 6-8 minutes, stirring, until thick.
Top with the honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the oats in the milk and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring often, 8-10 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Top generously with the honey.
description: A simple breakfast porridge.

name: Romanian Sarmale (Cabbage Rolls)
steps: Blanch the cabbage leaves.
Roll with the rice and beef mixture inside.
Simmer in the tomatoes and water, covered, 40-45 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: whole leaves if available, standing in for whole sour/pickled cabbage leaves

steps_detailed: Mix the raw rice with the beef, pork, and onion.
Wrap portions of the filling in the sauerkraut leaves (or shredded sauerkraut pressed around the filling if whole leaves aren't available), rolling snugly.
Pack into a pot, pour the tomatoes and a splash of the sauerkraut's own brine over, cover, and simmer 45-50 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and the rolls are tender.
description: Real Romanian sarma is built on sour, fermented cabbage leaves specifically, not fresh cabbage - that tang running through the whole dish, distinct from the sweeter, plainer flavor fresh cabbage gives, is what actually defines sarma against a generic stuffed-cabbage-roll recipe. A mix of beef and pork is also more traditional than beef alone.

name: Romanian Ciorba (Sour Beef Soup)

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Add the potato and 2 cups water, and simmer 30-35 minutes until tender.
Finish with the lime juice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion and garlic over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add the potato and 2.25 cups water, and simmer 32-38 minutes until fully tender.
description: Finish with the lime juice - ciorba is traditionally soured with bors, a fermented wheat bran liquid unique to Romanian cooking; lime carries the sour note here instead.

name: Romanian Mamaliga with Sour Cream
steps: Simmer the cornmeal in the milk and a little water, stirring constantly, 12-15 minutes until thick.
Stir in the butter and top with the sour cream.
steps_detailed: Simmer the cornmeal in the milk and a little water over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 14-17 minutes until thick and smooth.
Stir in the butter and cheese until melted, and top generously with the sour cream.
description: Mămăligă with sour cream, a simple, classic Romanian pairing.

name: Romanian Chickpea Soup

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Simmer the chickpeas with the onion, tomatoes, and caraway for 15-18 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Simmer the chickpeas, onion, garlic, tomatoes, and caraway together over medium-low heat, 16-20 minutes, mashing some of the chickpeas to thicken.
description: A simple chickpea soup.

name: Romanian Mici (Grilled Meat Rolls)
steps: Mix the beef with the garlic, caraway, and pepper, and shape into small finger-shaped rolls.
Grill or pan-fry, 8-10 minutes, turning, until cooked through.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef with the garlic, caraway, pepper, and baking powder thoroughly, and shape into small finger-shaped rolls.
Grill or pan-fry over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes, turning, until well browned and cooked through.
description: Mici, garlicky grilled meat rolls, are the centerpiece of Romanian outdoor cookouts, traditionally a mix of beef, pork, and lamb.

name: Romanian Pork and Sauerkraut
steps: Brown the pork with the caraway and pepper.
Add the sauerkraut and a splash of water, and simmer 25-28 minutes until tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the pork with the onion, caraway, and pepper over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add the sauerkraut and a generous splash of water, and simmer 26-30 minutes until the pork is fully tender.
Varza calita, pork simmered with sauerkraut, is a comforting Romanian winter staple.
description: Pork and sauerkraut, a classic Romanian and wider Central European pairing.

name: Romanian Beef and Cabbage Stew

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Add the cabbage and tomatoes, and simmer 30-35 minutes until tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion and garlic over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add the cabbage and tomatoes, and simmer 32-38 minutes until fully tender.
description: A simple beef and cabbage stew.

name: Romanian Cheese and Potato Bake
steps: Layer the potato with the sour cream in a small dish and bake at 375F, covered, for 25 minutes.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 10-12 more minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Layer the potato with the sour cream, butter, and pepper in a small baking dish, cover, and bake at 375F (190C) for 28-30 minutes until nearly tender.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 12-14 more minutes until golden and bubbling.
description: A simple cheese and potato bake.

name: Romanian Mamaliga Fries
steps: Simmer the cornmeal in 1 cup water, stirring constantly, 12-15 minutes until very thick, then spread flat and chill until firm.
Cut into sticks and fry in the oil until golden, and top with the cheese.
steps_detailed: Simmer the cornmeal in 1.1 cups water over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 14-17 minutes until very thick, then spread flat in a dish and chill at least 1 hour until firm.
Cut into sticks, season with pepper, and fry in the oil, 2-3 minutes per side, until crisp and golden, topping with the cheese while still hot.
description: Fried mămăligă, a simple way to use up leftover cornmeal porridge.

name: Romanian Cheese and Dill Snack
steps: Arrange the cheese with the dill and a light spread of butter.
steps_detailed: Arrange the cheese with the dill, butter, and a little black pepper - simple and common alongside bread or mamaliga.
description: A simple cheese and dill snack.

name: Romanian Caraway Crackers
steps: Rub the butter into the flour and caraway.
Add the milk to form a firm dough, roll thin, cut into shapes, and bake at 375F for 12-15 minutes until crisp.
steps_detailed: Rub the butter into the flour, caraway, and pepper until crumbly.
Add the milk to form a firm dough, roll thin, cut into small shapes, and bake at 375F (190C) for 13-16 minutes until crisp and lightly golden.
description: A simple caraway cracker.

name: Romanian Chickpea and Onion Snack

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Toss the chickpeas with the onion, oil, and lime juice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Toss the chickpeas with the onion, oil, lime juice, and pepper.
Let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld before serving.
description: A simple chickpea and onion snack.

name: Romanian Papanasi-Style Fried Dough
steps: Mix the cheese, egg, and flour into a dough.
Shape into rounds and fry, 3-4 minutes per side.
Top with honey.

ingredients_full.note: standing in for brânză de vaci farmer's cheese
tbsp, standing in for fruit preserves

steps_detailed: Mix the cottage cheese with the egg and flour into a soft, slightly sticky dough - papanași are fundamentally a cheese dough, not a plain fried dough, and the cheese needs to actually be worked into the dough itself, not just served alongside.
Top with the sour cream and honey.
description: Shape into small rounds with a hole in the center (traditionally a bigger central ball plus small rounds around it) and fry in the oil over medium heat, 3-4 minutes per side, until deeply golden and cooked through in the center. Real papanași are traditionally topped with fruit preserves (usually blueberry or sour cherry) alongside the sour cream - honey stands in here since a matching jam isn't a distinct database item, but the cheese-based dough itself is the part that actually defines the dish, and that's now correctly represented.

name: Romanian Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon, honey, and butter over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.
description: A simple rice pudding.

name: Romanian Honey Cake
steps: Cream the butter and honey, beat in the egg, then fold in the flour.
Bake at 350F for 20-22 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and honey together until light, then beat in the egg.
Fold in the flour, cinnamon, and baking powder, pour into a small lined pan, and bake at 350F (175C) for 22-26 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
description: A simple honey cake.

name: Romanian Sour Cream Dill Sauce
steps: Mix the sour cream, dill, and garlic together.
steps_detailed: Mix the sour cream, dill, garlic, and pepper together - good over mamaliga, potatoes, or grilled meats.
description: A simple sour cream and dill sauce.

name: Romanian Garlic Sauce (Mujdei)
steps: Mash the garlic into a paste and whisk with the oil and lime juice.
steps_detailed: Mash the garlic into a smooth paste and whisk with the oil, lime juice, and pepper.
Mujdei, an intensely garlicky sauce, is served with roasted meats, fried mamaliga, or grilled fish throughout Romania.
description: Mujdei, a strong garlic sauce, a genuine Romanian condiment, especially alongside grilled meats.
